title: [ROOT-CAUSE CONFIRMED + PRIMARY FIX LANDED] Audit exit-81 'bridge artifact timeout' on cycles 904-911 was Fable 5 QUOTA EXHAUSTION misclassified — NOT difficulty-scaled deadlines. claude-tmux exhausted_regex did not match the per-model 'reached your Fable 5 limit' wording. Regex generalized (branch fix/tmux-exhaustion-regex-per-model-limit). Remaining: drift fail-loud backstop + wakeAt-empty downstream.
summary: CORRECTION to the original hypothesis. The 8 consecutive non-shipping audit cycles (904-911, all exit=81 'core: bridge artifact timeout', audit-report.md MISSING) were NOT heavy-diff/2-wide/difficulty-scaled-deadline timeouts. They were Fable 5 PER-MODEL QUOTA EXHAUSTION. Every failing cycle's audit-escalation-report.json final_pane shows Claude Code's per-model quota wall — the second-person 'reached-your-<MODEL>-limit' message plus its credits-purchase prompt (verbatim string intentionally NOT reproduced here: it is a live exhausted_regex trigger, and the raw checkpoint pane is unstripped, so committing the exact wall text into a file an agent may cat/grep would fast-fail a healthy reader — see exhaustion-checkpoint-raw-pane-stripping). The auditor launched on `--model fable`; Fable 5's per-model quota was drained (by the 877-899 livelock's endless Fable-heavy re-audits); Claude Code rendered the limit wall and parked at the prompt; the two idle-nudges hit a quota-blocked session (result=no_effect); after 600s the generic artifact-wait killed it exit=81; the orchestrator treated exit=81 as a transient infra timeout and self-heal-relaunched -> same drained quota -> identical failure (attempt_count=2, ~21 min wasted per cycle).
root_cause: CONFIRMED (evidence, not hypothesis): the claude-tmux manifest `controls.usage.exhausted_regex` only matched the LEGACY wording `reached your (usage|weekly) limit`. Claude Code (v2.1.212) now emits a PER-MODEL wall `You've reached your <Model> limit` (model name interpolated where 'usage'/'weekly' used to be), so the regex did NOT match. Because the pane never classified as panestream.LivenessExhausted, the ALWAYS-ON exhaustion fast-fail in driver_tmux_repl.go:677 (`if livenessState==LivenessExhausted -> return ExitUnknownPrompt/85 -> fallback CLI`) was BYPASSED. The phase fell through to deterministicReviewer.Review -> default case -> ReviewPause ('no output during the last 300s interval — stalled') -> artifact-wait FAIL (exit 81, NOT 85). Exit 81 is treated as transient -> relaunch -> repeat. The existing exhaustion unit test used a SYNTHETIC legacy-wording fixture (de-spaced here as 'reached-your-usage-limit' so this todo is not itself an exhausted_regex trigger) that matched the old regex and passed — false confidence that let the upstream wording drift go uncaught (same lesson as token-telemetry: verify detection patterns against REAL captured on-disk data, not fixtures). This is a detection-pattern-drift class bug, orthogonal to ADR-0072 (the floor correctly stayed quiet: the audit artifact was genuinely absent -> coherent -> not a forged verdict).
fix: PRIMARY (LANDED on branch fix/tmux-exhaustion-regex-per-model-limit): generalized the model-name slot to `reached your .{1,40}? limit` (matches any short model name incl. version numbers; non-greedy; newline-safe) and added the model-independent hard-wall anchor `run /usage-credits to continue`. Does NOT match the soft 'approaching your ... limit' warning (which would kill a working agent — the cycle-254/255 false-FAIL sin). New regression test manifest_exhaustion_wording_test.go asserts the REAL captured strings match; the e2e driver_tmux_repl_exhaustion_test.go fixtures updated from synthetic to the real per-model wording. bridge pkg green (vet + -race). REMAINING follow-ups: (1) exhaustion-regex DRIFT fail-loud backstop — see new inbox item exhaustion-regex-drift-failloud (when a phase dies exit-81 AND final_pane shows a quota-wall signal the regex missed, emit a LOUD diagnostic so the NEXT drift is caught in 1 cycle not 8); (2) the exit-85 -> all-families-exhausted -> quota-pause path writes an empty wakeAt (see quota-pause-wakeat-unpopulated 0.88) so a resumable pause has no reset hint.
acceptance: DONE: root cause confirmed via final_pane evidence across cycles 904-911 (Fable 5 quota wall, not deadlines/concurrency).
DONE: claude-tmux exhausted_regex matches per-model 'reached your <Model> limit' + '/usage-credits' wall; does not match the soft approaching-warning; regression test uses real captured strings.
REMAINING: an audit whose pane shows an unmatched quota-wall signal emits a loud drift diagnostic (exhaustion-regex-drift-failloud item).

VERIFY-ON-RESUME: after Fable 5 quota resets, a fresh cycle whose auditor hits the wall mid-phase now fast-fails exit=85 and fails over / pauses-resumably instead of burning the 600s artifact timeout twice.
files: go/internal/bridge/driver_tmux_repl.go:677 (the always-on exit-85 fast-fail that was being bypassed)
connects_to: quota-pause-wakeat-unpopulated (0.88 — downstream: exit-85 quota-pause writes empty wakeAt)
exhaustion-regex-drift-failloud (NEW — the durability backstop for the NEXT wording drift)
goal-stall-escalation (0.87) + adr0072-s5-task-quarantine (0.85 — the 877-899 livelock that DRAINED the Fable quota in the first place)
recover-false-fail-features-876-897-898 (0.9 — the regime-1b deliverable-authority false-FAILs that fed the livelock)
infra-teardown-predicate-single-source (0.7 — exit-81 vs exit-85 teardown classification)
notes: Superseded the original 'difficulty-scaled audit deadlines' hypothesis, which is REFUTED by the final_pane evidence. Weight dropped 0.9->0.6 because the primary blocker is fixed; remaining work is the drift backstop + wakeAt. The livelock (877-899, single goal_hash re-picked ~10x) is what drained the per-model quota — fixing goal-stall-escalation / task-quarantine prevents the quota drain that triggers this class.
